preview_text:
The model favors Morocco at 74%, leaving Haiti with a 5% chance of victory. This is a heavily asymmetric matchup in terms of win probability, though the 21% draw likelihood prevents the favorite from being entirely dominant.

The spread across outcomes reflects a match expected to be competitive in structure if not in outright upset probability. Morocco's near-3-to-1 advantage in win probability sits alongside a meaningful draw contingency—one in five outcomes—suggesting that while Morocco enters as clear favorite, a stalemate remains plausible. Haiti's win probability, though low, is not negligible at 5%.

The model and sportsbook consensus align closely across all three outcomes, with gaps under 3 percentage points. Polymarket prices show slightly tighter odds on Morocco and lower odds on a draw, but the three sources move together within a narrow band. No material divergence suggests disagreement on the fundamental shape of this fixture.
